goal: B2-6 owned/unlocked deck still renders dull on the Decks page after sign-in
root_cause: decks.js rendered lock state once (canUnlockDeck → is-locked → .decks-cell__thumb saturate(0.35) opacity(0.65)) and never re-rendered on auth change. So a deck that became unlocked after sign-in kept its stale is-locked/dull class until a manual reload. (Home strip had the same class of bug, fixed in #3.)
fix: decks.js init now subscribeAuthState(() => renderAll()) — re-renders the grid on every auth change so newly-unlocked decks drop the dull treatment. Audited canUnlockDeck: correct (moonmallow always; null-unlock_day/default decks unlock when logged in; streak decks need accumulated days).
verified: node --check passes; mirrors the proven home-strip #3 + profile auth-subscribe pattern. Logged-in visual refresh not harness-testable (no session) but logic verified. md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
